free_text: Energy and angular distributions of neutrons from 90
MeV and 140 MeV alpha-particle bombardment of nuclei
were measured with 12.7-cm diameter by 10.2-cm deep
NE-102 plastic scintillators. The low energy (<30 MeV)
portions of spectra were measured with 5.08-cm
diameter by 5.08-cm deep NE-213 liquid scintillators.
Both scintillators were coupled to photomultiplier
Thin NE-102 plastic scintillation counters were placed
in front of each neutron detector to veto charged
. neutron attenuation through air (up to 10 MeV),
. neutron attenuation through scattering chamber wall.
Data taken from tables in Kalend thesis.
No reply from B. Anderson to author proof.
Converted from data set O0141; updated
ERR-ANALYS changed and moved to subentries
Relative uncertainties were primarily statistical and
included contributions from the shadow-bar background
subtractions. They were typically a few percent and
small compared to the overall scale uncertainties.
Integrated over laboratory angles of measured
to more forward and backward angles
No details given about type of error
